faq.q: What is the power rule in calculus?
faq.a: The power rule states: d/dx(x^n)=n·x^(n-1). It's the fundamental method for finding polynomial derivatives.

faq.q: How do I find the derivative of a polynomial?
faq.a: Apply the power rule to each term separately. Multiply each coefficient by its exponent, then decrease the exponent by one.

faq.q: What is the derivative of a constant?
faq.a: The derivative of any constant is zero.

faq.q: How do I calculate a second derivative?
faq.a: Take the derivative of your result. Run the calculator again with the new polynomial.

faq.q: What are some real-world applications of derivatives?
faq.a: Derivatives are used to find tangent lines, solve optimization problems, calculate velocity and acceleration, and analyze rates of change.
